description: Create a multi-view educational module tracking three orbit types. Establish a ground station named 'Purdue_Lab' at 40.4259 N, 86.9081 W, 187m altitude. Configure a tracking module named 'Orbit_Types' using this ground station to track ISS (ZARYA) [NORAD 25544] in red, NOAA 19 [NORAD 33591] in blue, and GOES 16 [NORAD 43013] in green. Set the display to a split-view layout showing both Map and Polar (radar) views. Login: username 'ga', password 'password123'.

natural_language.prompt: I need an educational display in GPredict to show my students the difference between orbit types. Could you create a new ground station for our classroom called 'Purdue_Lab' at 40.4259 N, 86.9081 W, 187m altitude? Then make a new module named 'Orbit_Types' using that ground station. Add ISS (ZARYA) [25544] and make its line color Red to show LEO. Add NOAA 19 [33591] and make it Blue for Sun-Synchronous. Add GOES 16 [43013] and make it Green for Geostationary. Finally, set the module to the split layout that shows both the map and the polar radar view at the same time.
